prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(initialize-batch bulk_batch_1)
(schedule-batch-on-line-slot bulk_batch_1 line_slot_1)
(assign-process-step-to-batch bulk_batch_1 process_step_1)
(complete-batch-process-step bulk_batch_1 process_step_1)
(prepare-equipment-for-bulk-batch bulk_batch_1 equipment_1 process_step_1)
(assign-operator-to-batch bulk_batch_1 operator_1)
(confirm-equipment-and-operator-ready-for-bulk-batch bulk_batch_1 equipment_1 operator_1)
(release-process-step-assignment bulk_batch_1 process_step_1)
(release-operator-from-batch bulk_batch_1 operator_1)
(initialize-batch packaging_batch_1)
(schedule-batch-on-line-slot packaging_batch_1 line_slot_2)
(assign-process-step-to-batch packaging_batch_1 process_step_1)
(complete-batch-process-step packaging_batch_1 process_step_1)
(prepare-packaging-line-for-packaging-batch packaging_batch_1 packaging_line_1 process_step_1)
(release-process-step-assignment packaging_batch_1 process_step_1)
(assign-operator-to-batch packaging_batch_1 operator_1)
(confirm-packaging-line-and-operator-ready-for-packaging-batch packaging_batch_1 packaging_line_1 operator_1)
(release-operator-from-batch packaging_batch_1 operator_1)
(assemble-batch-record-from-line-and-equipment-assignments bulk_batch_1 packaging_batch_1 equipment_1 packaging_line_1 batch_record_1)
(assign-process-step-to-batch bulk_batch_1 process_step_1)
(finalize-batch-record-qc-section batch_record_1 bulk_batch_1 process_step_1)
(release-process-step-assignment bulk_batch_1 process_step_1)
(mark-bulk-batch-qa-approved bulk_batch_1 batch_record_1)
(mark-packaging-batch-qa-approved packaging_batch_1 batch_record_1)
(initialize-batch release_unit_1)
(schedule-batch-on-line-slot release_unit_1 line_slot_3)
(assign-process-step-to-batch release_unit_1 process_step_1)
(complete-batch-process-step release_unit_1 process_step_1)
(release-process-step-assignment release_unit_1 process_step_1)
(attach-quality-sample-to-release-unit-and-record release_unit_1 quality_sample_1 batch_record_1)
(assign-process-step-to-batch release_unit_1 process_step_1)
(process-quality-sample-and-mark-release-unit release_unit_1 quality_sample_1 batch_record_1 process_step_1)
(attach-calibration-certificate-to-release-unit release_unit_1 calibration_certificate_1)
(assign-operator-to-batch release_unit_1 operator_1)
(conduct-calibration-certificate-review-for-release-unit release_unit_1 calibration_certificate_1 operator_1 quality_sample_1 batch_record_1)
(attach-environmental-record-to-release-unit release_unit_1 environmental_record_1)
(link-environmental-record-and-prepare-release-unit-for-review release_unit_1 environmental_record_1 quality_sample_1 batch_record_1)
(finalize-release-unit-as-qa-approved release_unit_1)
(release-operator-from-batch release_unit_1 operator_1)
(detach-calibration-certificate-from-release-unit release_unit_1 calibration_certificate_1)
(detach-environmental-record-from-release-unit release_unit_1 environmental_record_1)
(attach-release-form-to-batch release_unit_1 release_form_1 process_step_1)
(complete-release-unit-release-and-free-resources release_unit_1 line_slot_3 release_form_1)
(initialize-batch release_unit_2)
(schedule-batch-on-line-slot release_unit_2 line_slot_3)
(assign-process-step-to-batch release_unit_2 process_step_1)
(complete-batch-process-step release_unit_2 process_step_1)
(release-process-step-assignment release_unit_2 process_step_1)
(attach-quality-sample-to-release-unit-and-record release_unit_2 quality_sample_2 batch_record_1)
(assign-process-step-to-batch release_unit_2 process_step_1)
(process-quality-sample-and-mark-release-unit release_unit_2 quality_sample_2 batch_record_1 process_step_1)
(attach-calibration-certificate-to-release-unit release_unit_2 calibration_certificate_1)
(assign-operator-to-batch release_unit_2 operator_1)
(conduct-calibration-certificate-review-for-release-unit release_unit_2 calibration_certificate_1 operator_1 quality_sample_2 batch_record_1)
(attach-environmental-record-to-release-unit release_unit_2 environmental_record_1)
(link-environmental-record-and-prepare-release-unit-for-review release_unit_2 environmental_record_1 quality_sample_2 batch_record_1)
(finalize-release-unit-as-qa-approved release_unit_2)
(release-operator-from-batch release_unit_2 operator_1)
(detach-calibration-certificate-from-release-unit release_unit_2 calibration_certificate_1)
(detach-environmental-record-from-release-unit release_unit_2 environmental_record_1)
(attach-release-form-to-batch release_unit_2 release_form_1 process_step_1)
(complete-release-unit-release-and-free-resources release_unit_2 line_slot_3 release_form_1)
(assign-process-step-to-batch bulk_batch_1 process_step_1)
(attach-release-form-to-batch bulk_batch_1 release_form_1 process_step_1)
(complete-bulk-batch-release-and-free-resources bulk_batch_1 line_slot_1 release_form_1)
(assign-process-step-to-batch packaging_batch_1 process_step_1)
(attach-release-form-to-batch packaging_batch_1 release_form_1 process_step_1)
(complete-packaging-batch-release-and-free-resources packaging_batch_1 line_slot_2 release_form_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem involves pharmaceutical batch release readiness. The goal is to release bulk_batch_1, packaging_batch_1, release_unit_1, and release_unit_2.


## Examining initial conditions  
- The task focuses on preparing pharmaceutical batches for release, including bulk_batch_1, packaging_batch_1, release_unit_1, and release_unit_2.  
- The first step is understanding the current state, particularly the line slots involved in the process.

## Examining initial conditions  
- Line slots 1, 2, and 3 are available for scheduling.  
- Process_step_1, operator_1, material_lot_1, and environmental_record_1 are also ready for use.  

## Checking resource availability  
- Bulk_batch_1 is assigned to equipment_1, and packaging_batch_1 to packaging_line_1.  
- Quality_samples 1 and 2 are available, linked to release_unit_1 and release_unit_2 respectively.  

## Reviewing documentation status  
- Batch_record_1 is unpopulated but shared between release_unit_1 and release_unit_2.  
- Regulatory docs 1 and 2, signatories 1 and 2, release_form_1, calibration_certificate_1, and validation_record_1 are available.  

## Assessing validation records  
- Validation_record_1 is available and linked to both release units, but validation_record_2 is linked to release_unit_2 yet not listed as available.  
- This discrepancy may affect the release process for release_unit_2.  

## Analyzing release process  
- None of the batches (bulk_batch_1, packaging_batch_1, release_unit_1, release_unit_2) are initiated, requiring initialization first.  
- Release involves stages like initiating, scheduling on line_slots, assigning process_steps, and completing stages, with shared resources like batch_record_1.  

## Exploring release requirements  
- To release, batches need release_form_attached, which requires QA approval, process_step assignment, and release_form availability.  
- QA approval for bulk_batch involves resources_ready, equipment_verified, batch_record_populated, and qc_section_completed.  

## Investigating release unit specifics  
- Release_units, being packaging_units, follow similar release steps but can finalize as QA-approved via specific processes.  
- Completing release requires production_item_stage_completed and additional checks like environmental record linkage.

## Continuing Release Process  
- After preparing equipment and lines, assemble batch_record_1 for bulk_batch_1 and packaging_batch_1, linking equipment_1 and packaging_line_1.  
- This step requires both batches to be resources_ready, equipment_prepared, and verified, marking batch_record_1 as populated.  

## Handling QC Sections  
- Re-assign process_step_1 to bulk_batch_1 to finalize the QC section for batch_record_1, ensuring bulk_resources_ready and populated status.  
- Once QC is completed, release process_step_1 from bulk_batch_1, leaving equipment and packaging sections unset in the standard process.  

## Approving Batches for QA  
- Mark bulk_batch_1 as QA-approved, requiring resources_ready, equipment_verified, populated batch_record, and completed QC section.  
- Similarly, mark packaging_batch_1 as QA-approved, leveraging the shared QC section, with line_verified and populated status.  

## Preparing for Release Units  
- Release operator_1 from packaging_batch_1 and free up line_slots 1 and 2, as line_slot_3 remains available for release_units.  
- Initiate release_unit_1 and release_unit_2, scheduling them on available line_slots and preparing for QA approval processes like finalize_release_unit_as_qa_approved.

## Examining Release Unit Dependencies  
- Releasing release_unit_1 frees line_slot_3 and makes the release_form available again, but process_step assignment is no longer needed for completion.  
- Release_unit_2 follows a similar process, using quality_sample_2, but environmental_record_1 is already attached to release_unit_1, limiting availability.  

## Investigating Environmental Record Constraints  
- Only one environmental_record_1 exists, creating a conflict as both release_units need it for QA approval via finalize_release_unit_as_qa_approved, which requires linked_env.  
- Alternative paths like finalize_release_unit_with_checks still require ready_for_final_review, tied to environmental record linkage, leaving no clear path without it.  

## Exploring Alternative QA Approval Routes  
- Paths to QA approval for release_unit_2, such as finalize_release_unit_after_environmental_review, rely on env_review_completed, which needs a linked environmental record.  
- Without available validation_record_2 or environmental_record, no direct QA approval path exists, and signoff routes also depend on ready_for_final_review.

## Exploring Environmental Record Flexibility  
- Attaching environmental_record_1 to release_unit_1 sets ready_for_final, but finalize_as_qa_approved only requires ready_for_final, not persistent linkage.  
- After qa_approved, environmental_record_1 can be detached and reused for release_unit_2, solving the single record limitation.  

## Investigating Calibration Constraints  
- Calibration_certificate_1, like environmental_record_1, is limited to one, initially attached to release_unit_1 for evidence_reviewed.  
- After evidence_reviewed, calibration_certificate_1 can be detached, allowing reuse for release_unit_2 without persistent linkage.  

## Checking Resource Availability  
- Validation_record_1 remains available, while validation_record_2 is linked but not available, though not needed for the current path.  
- Material_lot1 and regulatory_documents (1 and 2) are available, supporting the process without staging conflicts.  

## Planning Release Unit Sequencing  
- Process release_unit_1 to qa_approved, then detach environmental_record_1 and calibration_certificate_1 for reuse with release_unit_2.  
- After releasing release_unit_1, line_slot_3 becomes free, enabling release_unit_2 to proceed, leveraging shared resources like the release_form.

## Continuing Release Unit Process  
- Release_unit_1 undergoes steps like initializing, scheduling on line_slot_3, and processing quality samples with batch_record_1.  
- Calibration and environmental records are attached, reviewed, and later detached after QA approval, freeing resources like line_slot_3.  

## Managing Resource Reuse  
- After releasing release_unit_1, calibration_certificate_1 and environmental_record_1 are detached, enabling reuse for release_unit_2.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
